turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hi , james , how is james ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: hi , good to see the doctor .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: it's good to see james too . so the doctor knows the nurse told james about dax , the doctor would like to tell dax a little bit about james .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: sure .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: james is a 57 year old male with a past medical history significant for coronary artery disease and lupus , who presents today with joint pain . so james , what's what's going on ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah . james has been putting a , a few more miles on the bike each day and , uh , james's knees have been killing james , just more so in the last three or four days or so .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . is the knee pain in one knee versus the other , or both ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: both equally .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and , um , does the knee pain hurt more in the morning when james wakes up , or does the knee pain hurt more when james is walking around and that type of thing ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: usually when james gets up in the morning , james's knees are pretty stiff and sore , and then by the end of the day , james starts to feel the same knee pain sensation . kind of pretty much , like , uh , probably around dinner time or so .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and any other , any other joints bothering james , james's elbows , wrists , shoulders , anything like that ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , the joint pain is just the knees .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: just the knees . and has james noticed any swelling of james's knees , or any redness of james's knees ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: uh , james's knees look a little inflamed in the morning , and then in the afternoon , james's knees look the same but not as bad , not as noticeable .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and does james have any other symptoms with the knee pain ? does james have any other associated fatigue or fever or anything like that ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , no .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and no injury ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , no . just , uh , repetitive motion , riding the bike the everyday , riding the bike the everyday is part of james's workout , james just probably , uh , probably doubled the miles .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay , all right . well , tell the doctor a little bit about . uh , the doctor knows , it's been a while since the doctor has seen james . the doctor knows , in terms of james's heart disease , and the doctor knows james got the stent placed last year .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: um , the doctor knows they took james off the plavix , is james still taking james's aspirin every day ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: james is .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and how is james doing with james's crestor ? any problems with crestor ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , none whatsoever .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and any chest pain or shortness of breath ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: no ? okay . okay . all right , and in terms of james's lupus , the doctor has , you know , lupus is something that was kinda back in the , in the background for james , and the doctor has n't had to do a whole lot . you know , james's kidney function's been really good . um , any , any issues as far as james knows ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , nothing , not at least in the last year or so .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . the doctor thinks the doctor , the doctor might have to look into lupus a little bit further with the knee pain that james's having .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: but the doctor wan na go ahead and , um ... you know , the doctor knows that the nurse did a review of system sheet and the doctor and james just covered a lot here .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: any other symptoms , abdominal pain , nausea , vomiting ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , no .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: no , just the knee pain ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . all right . well , let's go ahead and do a quick physical exam .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon , show the doctor the vital signs . so james's vital signs here in the office look really good .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: there's no fever , james's blood pressure and james's heart rate look great , so james's blood pressure and james's heart rate being great is , that's awesome .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: um , the doctor is just gon na check out james's heart and james's lungs .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: sure .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: and james's knees , and the doctor will let james know what the doctor finds , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: all right . so on physical examination of james's heart , you know , the doctor do hear that's , like , two out of six systolic ejection murmur , but the doctor has heard the two out of six systolic ejection murmur in the past . james's lungs sound nice and clear . and on james's knee exam , the doctor do appreciate , you know , some bilateral , uh , edema and some slight erythema , there is a slight effusion present on the right knee . does the right knee effusion hurt ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: the right knee effusion hurts a little bit .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: all right , so pain to palpation of the right knee . um , and decreased range of motion of the knees bilaterally . so the doctor just wan na look at some of james's results , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon . show the doctor the right knee x-ray . so the right knee x-ray is an x-ray of james's right knee and in reviewing the results of the right knee x-ray , everything looks fine , there's no boney abnormality , there's no fracture , which is not surprising , based on the history , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon . show the doctor the ekg . and the ekg is james's most recent ekg which the doctor did before james came in , and the ekg all looks really good . so , um , so no concerns from a heart standpoint . so let's talk a little bit about , you know , the doctor's assessment and the doctor's plan for james .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so for james's first problem , james's , james's knee pain , you know , the doctor i'm concerned that the knee pain might be from , you know , a flareup of james's lupus , so the doctor wan na go ahead and order and autoimmune panel to kinda see where the doctor and james stand , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: sure .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon . order an autoimmune panel . for james's next problem , the coronary artery disease , the doctor think james's doing really well , james's ekg looks good , james's vital signs look good .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: the doctor i'm gon na go ahead and order a lipid panel and another echocardiogram , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon . order an echocardiogram . does james have any questions ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: james has no questions .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: all right , well , the nurse will be in soon and the nurse'll get james checked out , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: the doctor got it .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey , dragon . finalize the note .
